rulings.Case Title: RAIN O DAUGHERTY VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Records That Were Lodged Provisionally Under Seal By Respondents On May 29, 2015 And June 5, 2015
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 14, 2015, Line 4, PLAINTIFF RAIN DAUGHERTY's Motion To Seal Records That Were Lodged Provisionally Under Seal By Respondents On May 29, 2015 And June 5, 2015 Granted. All references to material protected by Penal Code sections 832.7 and 832.8 must be filed under seal pursuant to the procedures set forth in Rule 2.550 and 2.551. Copley Press, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (2006) 39 Cal.4th 1272, 1296 establishes that the Legislature did not intend for an officer to have to choose between protecting his rights via an action under the Peace Officers Bill of Rights and his right of confidentiality as set forth in the Penal Code. The analysis in Long Beach does not apply as this solely involves disciplinary actions, not criminal charges. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/js

rulings.Case Title: ITC FINANCIAL LICENSES, INC. VS. STAN SCHULDINER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Re Hearing On Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 14, 2015, Line 5, PETITIONER ITC FINANCIAL LICENSES, INC's Motion Re Hearing On Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award The motion is granted and the award is confirmed. Even if there were some service issues, Mr. Schuldiner opposes the petition to confirm on the merits. In addition, CCP 1290.4 does not require service of a summons and arbitration petitions can be heard on as little as 10-days notice under CCP 1290.2. The court continued the hearing for two weeks at Mr. Schuldiner's request. Mr. Schuldiner received more than enough time to respond. The award was binding and Mr. Schuldiner waived any argument that the arbitration clause did not cover the dispute. Mr. Schuldiner was the claimant who requested arbitration. ITC correctly cites Moncharsh v. Heily & Blasé (1992) 3 Cal.4th 1, 11, for the proposition that an error of law is not a basis to vacate an arbitration award. There is no basis to vacate the award/deny confirmation under CCP 1286.2. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/js

rulings.Case Title: RASH B GHOSH VS. PAUL STEINER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Deposition And Production Of Documents, And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $3,850.64
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 14, 2015, Line 12, DEFENDANT PAUL STEINER's Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Deposition And Production Of Documents, And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $3,850.64 Defendant's motion to compel Plaintiff's appearance at deposition is granted. Defendant's motion to compel production of documents is denied as moot. The court awards $915 in sanctions to defendant to be paid within 30 days of notice of entry of order. This is a discovery matter and the court will not provide a court reporter. If the parties desire a reporter, they shall meet and confer and agree upon one. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
